patterns.name: BANK_ACC
patterns.comment: Bank account number — 9 or more digits (covers 11-digit, 14-digit, 18-digit, any length). Was \b\d{14}\b which missed 11-digit accounts.

patterns.name: DP_ID
patterns.comment: DP ID starting with IN followed by any number of digits

patterns.name: TRADING_ID
patterns.comment: 6-7 digit trading client ID. Negative lookaround prevents false match inside a longer number.

patterns.name: PAN
patterns.comment: PAN card — 5 uppercase letters, 4 digits, 1 uppercase letter

patterns.name: AADHAR
patterns.comment: 12-digit Aadhaar, optionally space-separated in groups of 4 (e.g. 1234 5678 9012)

patterns.name: IFSC
patterns.comment: IFSC code — 4 uppercase letters, literal 0, 6 alphanumeric characters

patterns.name: CIF
patterns.comment: CIF number — keyword CIF/cif required. Digits of any length follow. Supports: CIF 12345678, CIF: 12345678, CIF no. 12345678

patterns.name: CORP_ID
patterns.comment: Corporate ID — keyword CORP/CORPID required. Value is alphanumeric with NO length limit (any length). Supports: CORP ID ABC123, CORP: XYZ001, CORPID AB9988XYZ

patterns.name: USER_ID
patterns.comment: User ID — keyword USER ID/UID required. Value is alphanumeric with any length. Supports: USER ID U12345, UID: 456789, userid uid112233

patterns.name: EMAIL_ADDRESS
patterns.comment: Email address — regex fallback. IndicNER is the primary source for emails.

patterns.name: PHONE_NUMBER
patterns.comment: Indian mobile number — 10 digits starting with 6, 7, 8, or 9. Negative lookaround prevents partial matches.
